channel.title: Pages on Marín's - Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Pages on Marín's - Blog

item.title: About
item.description:
Hey! I’m Marín Alcaraz. I care about clean design, well-crafted APIs, and tools that feel good to use.

Currently

  • Staff Engineer at Twilio - Segment, designing APIs for enterprise data delivery
  • Exploring AI-assisted development workflows
  • Mentoring engineers

Background

I studied Computer Systems Engineering at ITESM with a brief but awesome stint at EPITECH in France.

After college I went to the Recurse Center and had the time of my life building projects like a Dropbox clone and my own ad-blocker.

item.title: Projects / Toys
item.description:
Toys

Toys are something different from projects. A toy is a personal, improvised creation — sketched with code, mostly written by AI, shaped to solve a narrow need I have at the moment. They’re not meant to scale, not meant to impress. They’re the software equivalent of a doodle on a napkin: a private, single-use thing built for the enjoyment and utility of making it work for me. The Software Slop of some is the treasure of others. Source available upon request.
